sections.subtitle: Team Form
sections.text: Arsenal are in strong form with 3 wins and no losses in their last 5 matches, showing consistency and defensive solidity with only 3 goals conceded this season. West Ham, on the other hand, are struggling, with no wins in their last 3 matches and a poor defensive record, conceding 14 goals in 6 games.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head encounters, results are split with West Ham winning 1-0 at Arsenal and Arsenal dominating with a 5-2 victory away. Matches between these teams often see over 2.5 goals, with 6 out of 8 recent games exceeding this mark.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Arsenal rank highly in attack (2nd for goals scored) and defense (1st for least goals conceded), with a high corner count (7.5 per game). West Ham struggle defensively (19th in the league), which could be exploited by Arsenal's attacking prowess and ability to create big chances (4th in the league).

sections.subtitle: Key Players
sections.text: Gabriel Martinelli (Arsenal) is in form with 3 goals in the last 5 matches, while Martin Ødegaard has a strong record against West Ham. Jarrod Bowen (West Ham) remains a threat with 3 goals recently, but may struggle against Arsenal's defense.

halftimeSections.subtitle: First half overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half at the Emirates Stadium saw Arsenal take a narrow lead over West Ham United with a score of 1-0. Declan Rice found the net in the 38th minute with a well-taken right-footed strike, giving the home side the advantage. Arsenal dominated much of the game, controlling possession and creating more dangerous opportunities. West Ham, on the other hand, struggled to find their rhythm and failed to register a shot on target despite having a few entries into the final third.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance and statistics
halftimeSections.text: Arsenal's performance in the first half was marked by their overwhelming control of the ball and ability to penetrate West Ham's defensive lines. They created multiple big chances, though they missed one significant opportunity. Their dominance in aerial and ground duels showcased their physical edge over the visitors. West Ham, conversely, appeared outmatched in most areas, with limited possession and far fewer entries into the penalty area. They did manage to create one big chance but failed to convert, highlighting their lack of cutting edge in the final third.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players and contributions
halftimeSections.text: Declan Rice was the standout for Arsenal, not only scoring the crucial goal but also contributing defensively with clearances. Bukayo Saka also played a pivotal role, creating a big chance and consistently threatening down the wing. For West Ham, Lucas Paquetá showed moments of creativity with a key pass and involvement in their sole big chance created. However, their forward line, particularly Niclas Füllkrug, couldn’t capitalize on the limited opportunities they had, missing a significant chance.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The attack momentum throughout the first half heavily favored Arsenal, with sustained spells of pressure from early on. After a brief initial push from West Ham in the opening minutes, Arsenal quickly took control, building consistent attacking waves around the midway point of the half. Their momentum peaked leading up to the goal, with relentless pressure on West Ham's defense. West Ham had fleeting moments of attacking intent, but these were quickly snuffed out as Arsenal regained dominance, especially in the latter stages of the half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical insights and potential adjustments
halftimeSections.text: Arsenal’s high possession and ability to win duels suggest they are likely to continue pressing for more goals in the second half, potentially exploiting West Ham's defensive frailties further. Mikel Arteta might encourage his team to maintain their intensity and capitalize on missed opportunities from the first half. West Ham, under David Moyes or their current manager, will need to adjust their approach, possibly introducing more attacking substitutions or shifting to a counter-attacking setup to exploit any overcommitment from Arsenal. Their defense must tighten up to prevent further damage.
